highlights:
Data Scientist – Publicis Groupe
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Publicis Groupe
[...]
## Beskrivning
[...]
Company descriptionPublicis Groupe is one of the largest communications groups in the world. We are building the agency model of the future and take a holistic approach to our clients' challenges. By fusing together our media, creative, data and technology expertise, we are able to provide clients with integrated solutions, transformative ideas and compelling consumer experiences. We call it the Power of One. We are proud winner of many awards and nominations, both in Sweden and internationally. But nothing makes us prouder than to work with clients and brands such as Disney, Elgiganten, Mondelez, Volvo, VISA, Lantmännen, Nomad Foods, Lactalis, Midsona, Renault and many more. At Publicis, we put our people first. We are proud to be certified by Great Place to Work (2022, 2023, 2024), and have been recognized with a Magnet Award for our engaging culture (2023). Our goal is to become the leading workplace in our industry. We are based in a fantastic office in Odenplan, but also offer a flexible work policy.OverviewJoin Publicis Intelligence - a dedicated team of specialists in data, technology, insights, and analytics! At Publicis Intelligence, we are looking for a talented Data Scientist to become part of our team and help drive business results for our clients. As a Data Scientist, you will play a central role in leveraging data to uncover actionable insights and develop models that enhance our marketing campaigns. You will collaborate closely with client teams and other marketing experts to identify growth opportunities, optimize performance, and deliver measurable results. Responsibilities: Data Analysis: Conduct in-depth analysis of large datasets to identify patterns, trends, and correlations relevant to marketing objectives. Model Development: Build and deploy models and algorithms to forecast customer behavior, optimize campaign targeting, and measure marketing effectiveness. Testing and Evaluation: Design and execute A/B testing and other experiments to continuously refine marketing strategies. Collaboration: Work closely with all team members to translate business questions into data-driven solutions. Client Relations: Build strong, trusted relationships with key clients. Stay Updated: Stay current with the latest advancements in data science, machine learning, and marketing technology.QualificationsBachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Marketing, or a related field. Experience with cloud platforms (GCP and/or Azure). Proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages (e.g., SQL, Python, R). Knowledge of machine learning algorithms. Understanding of statistical concepts. Familiarity with digital media concepts and metrics. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
[...]
Publicis Groupe

# Senior AI and Data Engineer
[...]
Company descriptionPublicis Groupe is one of the largest communications groups in the world. We are building the agency model of the future and take a holistic approach to our clients' challenges. By fusing together our media, creative, data and technology expertise, we are able to provide clients with integrated solutions, transformative ideas and compelling consumer experiences. We call it the Power of One.We are proud winner of many awards and nominations, both in Sweden and internationally. But nothing makes us prouder than to work with clients and brands such as P&G, Disney, H&M, Mondelez, Volvo, VISA, Lantmännen, LEGO, Spotify, Perrigo, Renault and many more.At Publicis, we put our people first. We are proud to be certified by Great Place to Work (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025) and have won the Diversity Charter Sweden Awards in the category Mental Health (2025). We have also been recognized with a Magnet Award for our engaging culture (2023). Our goal is to become the leading workplace in our industry. We are based in a fantastic office in Odenplan and offer a flexible work policy.Overview As a Senior AI & Data Engineer at Publicis Intelligence, you will play a key role in our journey towards becoming a more AI-driven consulting and product organization. The role is highly hands-on and technical, while also having clear strategic impact. You will join a team with strong existing capabilities in data, analytics, MarTech and insights. Generative AI and agent-based solutions are the next major focus area in our development.You will take technical leadership within GenAI. This includes identifying opportunities, shaping solutions and driving implementations from idea to production. You will work closely with other agencies and technical stakeholders across the organization.Your mission is to design, build and deploy AI solutions that create real business value, both internally and for our clients. Internally, this means improving efficiency, automation, decision support and new ways of working. Externally, you will help develop AI-driven capabilities that strengthen our clients' businesses.You act as the bridge between business needs, strategic goals and technical delivery. You ensure that solutions are robust, scalable and commercially relevant.This role is based in Stockholm and involves close collaboration with teams in Sweden and Denmark, as well as with Publicis' global AI initiatives.Responsibilities
[...]
- You are responsible for developing and scaling AI and GenAI solutions that deliver clear, measurable business impact for both internal teams and clients. You own AI agents, copilots and LLM-based applications end to end, from design to production. The focus is on efficiency, scalability and the creation of new AI-driven products and services.
[...]
- Beyond hands-on delivery, you act as a technical advisor and enabler. You help teams and clients turn AI potential into concrete, implementable solutions. You also contribute to the development of new AI-driven products across data, analytics and MarTech.
[...]
- You play a key role in building AI maturity across the organization. This includes sharing knowledge, mentoring others and driving innovation within GenAI and MLOps. You help shape best practices that allow the organization to scale and evolve its AI capabilities over time.
[...]
- You have 5+ years of experience in Data Engineering, ML Engineering, Analytics Engineering, or similar technical roles, and are comfortable operating across the full lifecycle of data- and AI-drive systems.
[...]
- Extensive experience in designing data pipelines, and proficiency in SQL, Python, and spark.
[...]
- You have proven experience with cloud architecture and implementing scalable data infrastructure and solutions.
[...]
- You have a strong grasp of API design, system integration, and service-based architecture, and the ability to connect AI components into larger technical ecosystems.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent

All jobs
onsite
# Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Appier is seeking a Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) to join their Enterprise Solution Science Team. In this role, you will be responsible for the end-to-end development, from problem definition to model deployment, of cutting-edge AI agents for marketing, strategy, sales, and service applications. You will collaborate with product and ML engineering teams, conduct evaluations, and stay updated with the latest research in LLM and agentic AI.
## About the role
## About the Role
We are looking for a **Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)** to join the Enterprise Solution Science Team. This team focuses on applying cutting-edge ML technologies to real-world marketing problems by combining them with omnichannel customer data. In this role, you will be responsible for end-to-end development, from problem definition to driving model deployment into production.
## What You’ll Work On
* You will contribute to the design, development, and productionization of one or more enterprise AI agents, including:
* **Marketing Agent**: Empower marketers to improve conversion rates and drive revenue growth.
* **Strategy Agent**: Transform raw data into actionable, revenue-driving strategies, and high-potential audience segments.
* **Sales and Service Agent**: Drive sales conversion, increase average order value (AOV), deepen brand engagement and customer loyalty through real-time, 24/7 AI-powered support.
* Collaborate with product teams to translate product requirements into well-defined machine learning problems
* Work with ML engineers to deploy models into production environments
* Conduct offline and online evaluation, monitoring, and continuous optimization after deployment
* Stay up to date with the latest research and proactively propose innovative applications## What We’re Looking For (Minimum)
* Master’s or PhD degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, or related fields
* Experience in **machine learning**, **LLM**, or **software engineering** through research, internships, projects, or industry work
* Experience or strong interest in **LLM / agentic application development**, including:
* Skills / tools / function calling
* AI Protocol (MCP / A2A)
* Workflow orchestration
* Benchmark evaluation
* Strong mindset with analytical and problem-solving skills, and passion for learning cutting-edge technologies
* Familiarity with AI-powered development tools such as: **Claude Code**, **OpenAI Codex / Codex CLI**, **Cursor**, **Windsurf**, **GitHub Copilot**## What We’re Looking For (Preferred)
* Strong focus on quantitative metrics and systematic evaluation
* Industry experience in MarTech and passion for building customer-centric products
* Familiarity with agentic product development concepts such as:
* Context engineering
* Harness engineering
* Evaluation / testing workflows
* Human-in-the-loop workflows

results.title: Sr. Data Scientist, Programmatic Algorithms | CareerSync
highlights:
Sr. Data Scientist, Programmatic Algorithms | CareerSync
[...]
# Sr. Data Scientist, Programmatic Algorithms
[...]
We're seeking a Senior Data Scientist to serve as an embedded Data Scientist within our Programmatic Experience Group. You'll own the design and deployment of machine learning models that optimize yield, pricing, and inventory allocation at scale — sitting at the intersection of data science, platform engineering, and marketplace economics.
[...]
This is a high-craft, high-ownership individual contributor role. You'll work end-to-end: architecting the data pipelines that feed your models, engineering the features that drive performance, and deploying real-time inference systems that make decisions at speed. Your work directly determines how effectively Impact's programmatic marketplace balances advertiser performance with publisher monetization — making this one of the highest-leverage technical roles in the business.
[...]
- Design and deploy ML models that optimize auction pricing, bid shading, floor price setting, and yield across Impact's programmatic inventory.
[...]
- Design and own the data infrastructure that feeds programmatic models: event pipelines, feature stores, training datasets, and real-time feature serving.
[...]
- Real-Time Inference & Production ML
[...]
- Deploy models to production real-time inference environments; own latency, reliability, and throughput requirements for auction-time decision-making.
[...]
- Experience: 5+ years in data science, ML engineering, or quantitative research, with at least 2+ years building and deploying ML models in programmatic advertising, ad tech, marketplace optimization, or a closely related domain (e.g., real-time bidding, dynamic pricing, auction systems).
[...]
- Programmatic & marketplace depth: Demonstrated understanding of programmatic auction mechanics (RTB, header bidding, floor pricing, deal types, bid shading) and how ML can be applied to optimize outcomes across the supply-demand stack.
[...]
- Production ML engineering: Proven ability to take models from prototype to production independently — including real-time inference, monitoring, retraining pipelines, and SLO ownership.
[...]
- Data architecture: Experience designing and building data pipelines, feature stores, and training infrastructure for high-volume, low-latency ML systems.
[...]
- Strong Python and SQL; proficiency with ML libraries (scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, PyTorch/TensorFlow) and large-scale data tools (Spark, Kafka, or equivalent streaming/batch frameworks).
[...]
- Experience with real-time feature serving and low-latency model deployment (REST APIs, gRPC, or streaming inference).
[...]
- Familiarity with production ML workflows: model versioning, drift monitoring, A/B testing, evaluation, and retraining.
[...]
- Experience processing and modeling at programmatic data scale: high-cardinality auction logs, bid stream data, impression and click events.
[...]
- Experimentation rigor: Strong grasp of causal inference and experiment design in online, delayed-feedback environments (auction holdouts, switchback tests, variance reduction techniques).
[...]
- Direct experience with SSP, DSP, or exchange-side yield optimization — particularly floor price optimization, bid landscape modeling, or deal matching algorithms.
[...]
- Experience with contextual bandits, multi-armed bandits, or reinforcement learning applied to real-time decisioning problems.
[...]
- Knowledge of online learning and adaptive algorithms in production environments with non-stationary data distributions.
[...]
- Familiarity with privacy-preserving ML techniques relevant to programmatic (differential privacy, federated learning, cookieless attribution modeling).
[...]
- Experience with GCP tools (BigQuery, Vertex AI, Dataflow, Pub/Sub) and/or Databricks/Spark for large-scale event processing and model training.

- Use AI tools to accelerate development responsibly (e.g., drafting SQL/Python, iterating on prompts, summarizing findings), while applying rigorous testing and verification.

- RAG vs. fine-tuning vs. prompting/tools for use cases.

- Expert SQL skills (complex joins, window functions, CTEs, query optimization).

results.title: Data Scientist I | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist I | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Data Scientist I

Posted Jun 6, 2026

GumGum is the contextual-first technology leader transforming digital advertising with AI-powered data and media solutions. The Data Scientist I supports the development and deployment of Machine Learning and AI solutions to improve the relevance and value of ads across various platforms, focusing on building and evaluating ML models and collaborating with cross-functional teams.

### Responsibilities

- Support the translation of business and product requirements into data-driven analyses and ML solutions
- Partner with Engineering team members and senior Data Scientists to develop, test, and deploy ML models
- Conduct exploratory data analysis to inform feature development and modeling approaches
- Query, clean, and structure large datasets using SQL, Spark, and cloud data platforms
- Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models under guidance from senior team members
- Contribute to existing MLOps workflows for model training, deployment, and monitoring
- Help define and track Key Performance Indicators in our Business Intelligence tools to measure model performance and downstream impact
- Document analyses, models, and learnings to support knowledge sharing across the team
- Continuously learn new ML techniques, tools, and advertising-domain concepts

Skills

- Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Economics, Computer Science or a related quantitative or technical field
- 1–2+ years in a data-driven role (Analytics, Data Science, or similar) -- WE ARE OPEN TO RECENT GRADS WITH INTERNSHIP EXPERIENCE
- Experience applying ML and statistical methods to real-world datasets
- Experience collaborating with Engineering and/or Product teams
- Exposure to production ML systems or MLOps tools (MLFlow, Weights & Biases, etc,)
- Familiarity with advertising, marketplaces, or large-scale decision systems

### Benefits

- 401(k) retirement plan
- Participation in a bonus, commission, or stock incentive program

Company Overview

- GumGum is an Artificial Intelligence company that uses machine learning to extract value from digital content. It was founded in 2008, and is headquartered in Santa Monica, California, USA, with a workforce of 501-1000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship

- GumGum has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 9 in 2025, 13 in 2024, 6 in 2023, 15 in 2022, 15 in 2021, 9 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

# lululemon Applied AI/ML Scientist
[...]
Description & Requirements Who we are lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. That includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. About this team The Enterprise Data & AI team is a strategic and operational driver of growth for lululemon, owning and building the data and AI platforms and products that enable the enterprise to operate with intelligence at scale. The team leads the design and delivery of a trusted unified data foundation, advanced analytics capabilities, and AI solutions across lululemon's vertically integrated retail ecosystem, embedding strong data governance and responsible AI practices from the very beginning. By applying AI to critical business challenges and creating new, transformative AI solutions, the team helps reshape how lululemon operates. Through deep partnership with product, technology, and business teams, Enterprise Data & AI accelerates product innovation, unlocks measurable value, elevates guest and educator experiences, and drives enterprise efficiency. Core responsibilities As an Applied AI/ML Scientist, you design, develop, test, and optimize AI/ML solutions that address defined business problems, with an emphasis on applied modeling, experimentation, and translating advanced AI research into scalable, production-ready solutions. You work independently to design and adapt AI/ML approaches to build prototypes, and improve model performance, reliability, and efficiency. You use domain knowledge and collaborate with stakeholders and product teams to map the business problem to the right model and AI/ML solution approach. You own the AI solution implementation and partner with engineering teams to integrate solutions into the broader engineering system. You contribute to solution enhancements, validate outcomes, and continuously improve models based on real world performance. Examples of projects could be

- Designing and deploying machine learning and predictive/generative AI models for demand forecasting, optimization, personalization and search, intelligent automation, and multimodal content creation and enrichment

- Collaborating with product and engineering teams to embed AI models into end to end business workflows

AI/ML engineers

- Experience with ML implementation using commonly used tools such as Python, pyTorch, cloud ML platforms and libraries, open-source toolkits.

results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
[...]
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
[...]
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
[...]
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
[...]
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
[...]
impact (revenue growth, cost reduction, or product differentiation).
[...]
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
[...]
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
[...]
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).

Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.

Experience with responsible AI frameworks, model governance, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).

Track record of building AI developer platforms or shared ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams.

highlights:

Senior Data Scientist

🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
ll be doing
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
Mentor team members, review
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
bonus if you:
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.
[...]
Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.

into machine learning objectives, training signals, and

criteria, working closely with domain experts.

You architect new initiatives that integrate diverse data modalities, guiding the technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.

The position involves building prototypes with AI-assisted coding tools such as Claude Code and Cursor, making it perfect for those who thrive in a fast-paced, AI-driven environment.
[...]
You will create scalable creative assets using generative AI platforms, working closely with Brand/Design and Performance Marketing teams. Your work includes building prototypes, running rapid experiments, and developing workflows that democratize creative production across the organization.
[...]
We’re looking for a Generative Creative Technologist to join Intuit’s Marketing Futures Team, a group embedded in our broader marketing organization tasked with defining, piloting, and scaling AI‑enabled and future‑oriented marketing capabilities. You will push the boundaries of what’s possible in marketing creative by harnessing generative AI tools, including Adobe Gen Studio, Claude Cowork, GPT Frontier, Midjourney, VEO, and emerging platforms, to produce scalable, high‑quality visual and multimedia assets. You’ll partner with Brand/Design and Performance Marketing to deliver AI‑generated creative at scale, run rapid experimentation, and build repeatable workflows that democratize creative production across the org. We are looking for a design‑minded technologist who is an avid learner, experimenter, and builder ready to change how creative gets made. This role is equal parts creative vision and technical execution, you will regularly take ideas from zero to working prototype, proving out what’s possible before it scales.
[...]
Generate scalable creative assets including images, video, audio, and interactive content using generative AI platforms (Claude Cowork, Cursor, GPT Frontier, Adobe Gen Studio, Adobe Firefly, Runway, Midjourney, VEO, Flow, Kling, Sora, Profound (GEO), and emerging tools).
[...]
from 0
[...]
Drive AI‑generated creative from pilot into live, in‑market campaigns and measure impact against real business outcomes: incremental subscribers, conversion rate lift, CAC efficiency, and LTV growth. Own the path from prototype to performance, partnering with Performance Media and Analytics to validate that AI creative is not increasing campaign velocity, but measurably better at driving personalization at scale (revenue/retention).
[...]
Design and execute rapid A/B/C testing programs for AI‑generated creative, measuring lift in CTR, CVR, and engagement across channels.
[...]
Collaborate with paid media teams to test AI‑generated creatives across Meta, Google, TikTok, and programmatic platforms; integrate with platform APIs for real‑time performance filtering.
[...]
4‑5 years of experience in creative production, design technology, or digital marketing, with at least 2 years of hands‑on experience using generative AI tools for creative output.

Experience building AI‑based prototypes using Claude Code, Cursor, Loveable or similar tools and going from prototype to MVP leveraging AI tech in marketing and sales functions.
[...]
Demonstrated proficiency with AI image, video, and audio generation tools (e.g., Midjourney, Runway, VEO, Nano Banana, GPT Image 2.0, Flow, Kling, Sora, Higgsfield Seedance, Stable Diffusion).
[...]
Proficiency in Python and experience using AI‑assisted coding tools (e.g., Claude code, Cursor, Loveable, v0/Vercel) to quickly prototype ideas and build working creative applications from scratch.
[...]
Experience with creative AI tooling and platforms (e.g., Adobe Gen Studio, ComfyUI, Weavy, artlist.io) for building and managing generative creative workflows.

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights: Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief periods of time. No significant stooping is usually required. Summary About Us Xsolis is an AI-driven technology company with a human-centered

results.title: [Hiring] Data Scientist REMOTE USA - Gulf Career Hunt
highlights:
[Hiring] Data Scientist REMOTE USA - Gulf Career Hunt

24 May 2026

Position: Data Scientist

Date Posted: May 24, 2026

Industry: Data Science / Market Research / Analytics / FinTech

Employment Type: Full Time

Experience: Not Specified

Qualification: Not Specified

Location: United States (Remote)

Company: YipitData

Description:

YipitData is a leading market research and analytics firm specializing in the disruptive economy. The company transforms massive alternative data sets into actionable insights used by top investment funds and Fortune 500 organizations to support high-impact financial and strategic decisions.

The company is currently hiring a Data Scientist to work on advanced analytical projects involving causal inference, predictive modeling, and large-scale alternative data processing. This role is highly technical and combines traditional data science with AI-native workflows, including the use of LLM-based tools to accelerate research, coding, and analysis.

The ideal candidate will work end-to-end on complex data science problems, translating ambiguous business questions into structured analytical solutions while producing high-quality insights, models, and written research outputs for institutional clients.

Key Responsibilities:

• Translate complex business questions into structured data science problems.

• Build and analyze predictive and causal models using large-scale datasets.

• Work with alternative data sources including transaction, invoice, and web-scraped data.

• Develop feature engineering pipelines using tools like Spark.

• Produce technical white papers and analytical reports for clients.

• Use LLM tools to enhance coding, analysis, and workflow efficiency.

• Build internal AI-driven tools and evaluation systems.

• Collaborate with engineering, product, and business teams on data solutions.

• Establish and maintain high technical standards for data science workflows.

Requirements:

• Proven experience delivering end-to-end data science projects with measurable impact.

• Strong understanding of causal inference and statistical modeling techniques.

• Advanced Python programming skills with clean, production-quality code.

• Experience working with large datasets and distributed computing tools.

• Strong writing and communication skills for technical documentation.

• Familiarity with LLM tools and AI-assisted development workflows.

• Strong knowledge of:

• Causal Inference and Statistical Modeling

• Predictive Analytics and Machine Learning

• Python, Spark, and Data Engineering Tools

Disclaimer: The job details above are structured for clarity and based on publicly available content from recruiters/Company pages. All rights remain with the original source; names may be withheld for confidentiality. We are not involved in the hiring process.

results.title: Senior Scientist, AI Applications - R&D Immunology Data Science & Digital Health
highlights:
# Senior Scientist, AI Applications – R&D Immunology Data Science & Digital Health Full-time
[...]
## Senior Scientist, AI Applications
[...]
Johnson & Johnson Innovative Medicine is currently seeking a Senior Scientist, AI Applications to join our Immunology R&D Data Science & Digital Health team (DSDH). This position will be located on site at one of our offices in either Spring House PA, Cambridge MA, Titusville NJ, Raritan NJ, or San Diego CA (NO fully remote option available).
[...]
The R&D Immunology Data Science and Digital Health (DSDH) team is seeking a Senior Scientist, AI Applications – Immunology to work closely with the Machine Intelligence (MI) team within DSDH AI/ML to develop and deliver artificial intelligence (AI)/machine learning (ML) scientific applications. This must be a mission-driven Scientist aimed at developing the future of autonomous AI in Immunology drug discovery and development. You'll work with Large Language Models (LLMs) and sophisticated data science techniques, harnessing massive datasets spanning scientific literature, omics, and biomolecular knowledge. Your work will directly accelerate research across the drug development pipeline, including novel target discovery, translational research and clinical trials—making reasoning agents faster, more accurate, and more reliable. Key focus areas include multimodal reasoning, agent robustness and uncertainty, agent memory architecture, and agent motivation frameworks. This role will transform AI/ML needs into actionable science, and work hands on to deliver decision-driving results.
[...]
- Submit Profile
- An individual contributor position focused on hands-on technical delivery of advanced analytic (AI/ML) Immunology R&D research.
- Work with immunology R&D and Data Science business leaders to translate business opportunities into hands on research plans.
[...]
- Develop and optimize multimodal reasoning systems that integrate large-scale structured and unstructured data to derive actionable conclusions
[...]
- Build novel agent memory architectures to enable persistent knowledge retention and context-aware decision-making across extended discovery workflows
[...]
- Prototype and evaluate innovative approaches for uncertainty quantification and reliability assessment in AI-driven scientific reasoning
[...]
- Serve as a domain expert within the field by articulating to team members emerging approaches and methodologies within the AI/ML with a clear understanding of their practical applications within the industry
[...]
- Benchmark agent performance against human expert decisions and existing computational methods and demonstrate ability to publish results in leading journals and conferences
[...]
- Define and enforce engineering best practices across AI prototyping, including architecture patterns, documentation standards, quality gates, and reproducibility criteria
[...]
- Serve as a key contributor to the over-arching AI strategy and to enable the scaling and deployment of tools aimed at the broader R&D community.
[...]
## Required qualifications:
[...]
- A Ph.D. degree in AI/ML or related field (e.g., computer science, machine learning, data science, applied mathematics, statistics) with at least two (2) years working experience
- Knowledge in sophisticated mathematics areas such as Bayesian inference, uncertainty quantification, information theory, causal inferencing, graph neural networks, etc.
[...]
- Proficiency with open-source agentic frameworks (e.g., LangGraph, DSPy, mem0, etc.) and deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, Tensorflow, etc.)
[...]
- Proficiency in one or more programming languages – preferably Python
[...]
- Understanding of the drug discovery pipeline and familiarity with biological data types
- Experience in Immunology research
- Strong understanding of statistics and applicable methods to validate model performance
- Familiarity with cloud hosting solutions (ex. AWS and/or Azure)
